headline: GREENLAND LOCKED IN: Critical Metals Secures 92.5% of Tanbreez HREE Deposit With $120M EXIM Backing
snippet: The Greenland government approved transfer of the final 50.5% stake in Tanbreez to Critical Metals Corp. (NASDAQ: CRML), bringing its ownership to 92.5% of a deposit containing all eight heavy rare earths essential for defence and clean energy. Pilot plant construction starts now; a 150-tonne bulk sample is planned for June, backed by a $120M U.S. EXIM Bank letter of intent.

headline: NEWMONT SHATTERS RECORDS: $3.1B Quarterly Free Cash Flow and $6B Buyback as $4,700+ Gold Pays Off
snippet: Newmont posted an all-time quarterly record $3.1B free cash flow in Q1 2026 — even after $1.3B in cash tax payments — as revenue jumped 46% YoY to $7.31B, beating the $6.53B consensus. Adjusted EPS of $2.90 crushed the $2.18 estimate. A $6B buyback announcement signals management believes current gold prices are structural, not transient.

headline: SILVER SYSTEM OPEN AT DEPTH: GR Silver Hits 351 g/t Over 15.6m at San Marcial, 500m Down-Dip Step-Out
snippet: GR Silver Mining's Plomosas step-out returned 15.6m at 351 g/t Ag — including 2.5m at 1,395 g/t — confirming high-grade silver 500 metres down-dip from surface. A newly identified andesitic dyke graded 1.2m at 1,618 g/t Ag, opening potential above the main breccias. The lower zone remains open at depth 75m below the high-grade interval, with 20,000m planned and two rigs running.

headline: THIRD PORPHYRY CONFIRMED: Arras Minerals Hits 115.7m at 1.44 g/t Au and 0.24% Cu at Elemes, Kazakhstan
snippet: Arras Minerals (TSXV: ARK) hit 115.7m at 1.44 g/t Au and 0.24% Cu at the Berezski East target on its 531 km² Elemes Project — confirming a third porphyry centre on the Berezski Trend. Gold-copper mineralisation extends from surface to 400m+ depth, and strong results are prompting an expansion from 10,000m drilled in 2025 to a 30,000m program by end of 2026.

headline: 99% GOLD RECOVERY AND C$42M RAISED: NevGold Validates Metallurgy at Nevada Antimony-Gold Project
snippet: NevGold Corp posted 99% phase II gold recovery at Limousine Butte, Nevada, alongside antimony intercepts up to 1.11% Sb over 6.1m, then upsized its brokered placement to C$42.2M — reflecting institutional appetite for a Nevada-jurisdiction antimony-gold story as American critical minerals sourcing draws strategic capital.

headline: RECORD NEVADA MINING RATE: Integra Resources Posts 12,635 oz Gold at Florida Canyon, Cash Hits $105M
snippet: Integra Resources (TSX: ITR) set a record mining rate of 76,800 tonnes per day at Florida Canyon in Q1 2026, producing 12,635 oz gold with ~3,000 oz deferred, and raised $61M via bought deal to close the quarter with $105.6M cash. Full-year guidance of 70,000–75,000 oz gold remains intact.

headline: MADSEN RAMPS TO FULL PRODUCTION: West Red Lake Gold Issues 2026 Guidance After January Commercial Production
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines achieved commercial production at the Madsen Mine in Ontario in January 2026 and has issued 2026 guidance, targeting approximately 60% of annual output in H2 as the operation scales to full run-rate from active stopes. Capital is allocated to underground development and satellite deposits earmarked for production between 2027 and 2029.

headline: SAUDI MINING PIVOT: Kingdom's Investment Vehicle Abandons Global M&A to Prioritise Domestic Development
snippet: A Saudi-linked mining investment vehicle is abandoning plans to acquire foreign mining assets as the kingdom pivots toward domestic economic development — removing a well-capitalised strategic acquirer from global mining M&A at a time when critical minerals consolidation has been running at multi-year highs.

headline: COPPER FORECAST SPLIT: Goldman Holds $12,650/t for 2026 While Traxys CEO Calls $15,000 Within Three Years
snippet: Goldman Sachs held its 2026 copper average forecast at $12,650/t despite projecting a 490,000-tonne surplus, noting concentrate shortages and China's sulphuric acid export ban. Trading firm Traxys is sharply more bullish: CEO Mark Kristoff called $15,000/t within 24–36 months at the FT Commodities Summit, pointing to structural deficits compounding through the decade.
